title: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.24 — Cisco ISE Certificate Hygiene Evidence (NAC PKI Lifecycle)
exclusions: Excludes test/sandbox/lab ISE deployments via the `ise_cert_audit_exceptions` lookup; only production ISE PKI is evidenced.
evidence: Saved search results archived to `audit_evidence` with ISO 27001 control tagging; scheduled PDF/CSV exports for auditor consumption; Splunk dashboard screenshots with timestamp watermarks capturing control operating effectiveness over the review period.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.24 requires controls over cryptographic lifecycle. UC-17.1.31 produces continuous machine-readable evidence of NAC certificate, CRL refresh, and OCSP health.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrate, at any audit instant, that ISE NAC certificates remain unexpired, that the trust chain validates, that CRL refresh succeeds, and that OCSP responders are reachable — for both the inner ISE PKI and any externally-anchored CAs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.56' running on `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`, archived to the `audit_evidence` index (default 7-year retention) with `iso27001_clause=A.8.24` tagging.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Set an ISE cert to expire in 50 days. UC-22.6.56 fires; evidence row appears in `audit_evidence` with `iso27001_clause=A.8.24`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All certs healthy. Search produces zero results; no false-positive evidence.

dataSources: `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Certificate* category — codes 36000-36050. Cross-reference UC-17.1.31 for the underlying detection logic.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) (Splunkbase) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (36003, 36004, 36005, 35201, 35102) earliest=-1d
| stats count by MessageCode, certificate_subject
| eval iso27001_clause="A.8.24", control="Cryptography"
| eval evidence_status=case(MessageCode=="36003", "cert_expiring_60d", MessageCode=="36004", "cert_expiring_30d", MessageCode=="36005", "cert_expired", MessageCode=="35201", "crl_refresh_failed", MessageCode=="35102", "ocsp_unreachable")
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.24 (cryptography) by collecting Cisco ISE certificate, CRL, and OCSP health events.
value: ISO auditors need proof that the NAC PKI is being actively managed, not just that policies exist. This UC takes the operational ISE alerts from UC-17.1.31 and re-routes them into the audit-evidence index with clause tagging, providing the auditor with continuous, machine-attested evidence at A.8.24 level.
implementation: (1) Deploy UC-17.1.31 first (operational base). (2) Add this wrapper to forward findings into `audit_evidence`. (3) Tag evidence with `iso27001_clause=A.8.24`. (4) Retain for the audit cycle (typically 3y for ISO 27001).
visualization: Compliance dashboard panel: cert-expiry trend; OCSP/CRL availability heatmap; A.8.24 control status single-value.

references.title: UC-17.1.31 — Cisco ISE Certificate Expiry and Trust-Chain Health

knownFalsePositives:
1. Expired test/lab certificates — exclude via cert subject CN matching.
2. Brief OCSP responder maintenance windows.

**Suppression mechanism:** Maintain an `ise_cert_audit_exceptions` KV Store lookup with cert CN allowlist for non-production systems.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.31 deployed and producing 36003/36004/36005/35201/35102 events.
- `audit_evidence` index exists with 3y minimum retention and ACL-restricted access for compliance team only.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Ingestion is via Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915) — already covered by UC-17.1.31's deployment.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

The search is the same logic as UC-17.1.31 but tagged with `iso27001_clause=A.8.24` and routed to `audit_evidence` rather than producing operational notables. This separation means operations sees alerts (with their own retention) and compliance sees evidence (with audit retention).

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.6.56: ISO 27001 A.8.24 — ISE Cert Hygiene Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.6.56,reg=ISO27001,clause=A.8.24
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

(a) Confirm UC-17.1.31 is producing data: `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog MessageCode IN (36003,36004,36005,35201,35102) | head 10`.
(b) Confirm evidence is reaching the audit index: `index=audit_evidence uc=22.6.56 | stats count by evidence_status`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

Build a compliance dashboard panel titled 'ISO 27001 A.8.24: ISE PKI Lifecycle' showing: (1) Cert-expiry trend, (2) OCSP/CRL availability heatmap, (3) A.8.24 control status single-value.

## Step 5 — Audit submission

Produce monthly evidence packs by exporting `index=audit_evidence uc=22.6.56` for the requested period as PDF/CSV. Annotate with control owner attestation and submit as ISO Stage 2 / surveillance audit evidence.
grandmaExplanation: We continuously evidence ISO 27001 A.8.24 (cryptography) by collecting Cisco ISE certificate, CRL, and OCSP health events. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
